title: Counting Element Occurrences in a Tuple

content.problemStatement: Imagine you are developing a data analysis tool that helps users find how many times a specific element appears in a tuple. Instead of manually searching, the program will take user input to create a tuple and a specific element to count, then use Python’s count() method to determine its occurrences.
constraints: Each tuple contains at most 15 elements
Each element string length after trimming is at most 100 characters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case there are two lines: the first line is a comma‑separated list of elements (each element may contain spaces which should be trimmed), the second line is the element whose occurrences need to be counted.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line in the exact format: The element '<element>' appears X time(s) in the tuple.

sampleTestCases.input:
1
apple, banana, apple, mango, banana, apple
apple
sampleTestCases.output: The element 'apple' appears 3 time(s) in the tuple.
sampleTestCases.explanation: The tuple after trimming is [apple, banana, apple, mango, banana, apple]; 'apple' occurs 3 times.

sampleTestCases.input:
2
red, blue, green, red, yellow
red
cat, dog, mouse, cat, cat, rabbit
dog
sampleTestCases.output:
The element 'red' appears 2 time(s) in the tuple.
The element 'dog' appears 1 time(s) in the tuple.
sampleTestCases.explanation: First test case: 'red' appears twice. Second test case: 'dog' appears once.

sampleTestCases.output: The element 'hello' appears 1 time(s) in the tuple.
sampleTestCases.explanation: Single element tuple matches the search element.

hidden.label: minimum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput: The element '' appears 0 time(s) in the tuple.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.input:
1
elem1, elem2, elem3, elem4, elem5, elem6, elem7, elem8, elem9, elem10, elem11, elem12, elem13, elem14, elem15
elem10
hidden.expectedOutput: The element 'elem10' appears 1 time(s) in the tuple.

hidden.label: edge case with spaces
hidden.expectedOutput: The element 'foo' appears 4 time(s) in the tuple.

hidden.label: general case 1 (two test cases)
hidden.input:
2
apple, banana, apple, mango, banana, apple
apple
red, blue, green, red, yellow
red
hidden.expectedOutput:
The element 'apple' appears 3 time(s) in the tuple.
The element 'red' appears 2 time(s) in the tuple.

hidden.label: case sensitivity
hidden.expectedOutput: The element 'apple' appears 2 time(s) in the tuple.

solution.approach: For each test case read the whole line containing the tuple and split it by commas. Trim leading and trailing spaces from each token and from the search element. Compare the trimmed token with the trimmed search element and increase a counter when they are equal. Finally print the required sentence. The algorithm is linear in the number of elements of the tuple.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) where N is the number of elements in a tuple (N ≤ 15).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the input strings.
